     The Numbers Business Report

Dr. Seuss’ The Grinch (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $273,468,675Details
International Box Office $241,774,702
Worldwide Box Office $515,243,377
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $36,933,565
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $30,436,709
Total $67,370,274
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Each year at Christmas they disrupt his tranquil solitude with their increasingly bigger, brighter and louder celebrations. When the Whos declare they are going to make Christmas three times bigger this year, the Grinch realizes there is only one way for him to gain some peace and quiet: he must steal Christmas. To do so, he decides he will pose as Santa Claus on Christmas Eve, even going so far as to trap a lackadaisical misfit reindeer to pull his sleigh. Meanwhile, down in Who-ville, Cindy-Lou Who-a young girl overflowing with holiday cheer-plots with her gang of friends to trap Santa Claus as he makes his Christmas Eve rounds so that she can thank him for help for her overworked single mother. As Christmas approaches, however, her good-natured scheme threatens to collide with the Grinch's more nefarious one. Will Cindy-Lou achieve her goal of finally meeting Santa Claus? Will the Grinch succeed in silencing the Whos’ holiday cheer once and for all?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$67,572,855 (24.7% of total gross)
Legs:4.05 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,141 opening theaters/4,141 max. theaters, 7.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $357,209,667

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 9th, 2018 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: November 7th, 2018 (Wide) (Egypt)

November 8th, 2018 (Wide) (Cyprus)

November 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Brazil)

MPA Rating: PG for brief rude humor.

Running Time: 86 minutes

Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Single Parent, Widow/Widower, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Release detail: Christmas in November
Social setting: Christmas
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Illumination Entertainment, Chris Meledandri
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Each year at Christmas they disrupt his tranquil solitude with their increasingly bigger, brighter and louder celebrations. When the Whos declare they are going to make Christmas three times bigger this year, the Grinch realizes there is only one way for him to gain some peace and quiet: he must steal Christmas. To do so, he decides he will pose as Santa Claus on Christmas Eve, even going so far as to trap a lackadaisical misfit reindeer to pull his sleigh. Meanwhile, down in Who-ville, Cindy-Lou Who-a young girl overflowing with holiday cheer-plots with her gang of friends to trap Santa Claus as he makes his Christmas Eve rounds so that she can thank him for help for her overworked single mother. As Christmas approaches, however, her good-natured scheme threatens to collide with the Grinch's more nefarious one. Will Cindy-Lou achieve her goal of finally meeting Santa Claus? Will the Grinch succeed in silencing the Whos’ holiday cheer once and for all?
